table:08FAE:40: Saving here adds a "Voice Cast" option to the opening config menu. Select it to hear messages from Langrisser's star-studded cast.

table:0D7F6:232: A lesser dragon, all red. Its intelligence

table:0D7F6:234: they say. But it is slow.

table:0FC9A:25: fairly cheap, for novice to expert.
table:0FC9A:26: Good for thrust and cut, the most popular

table:0FC9A:34: Of non-magic swords, it has top

table:0FC9A:37: Its icy blade is made of never-melting
table:0FC9A:40: A magic sword with a plasma blade extending from the hilt. It seals the power of a thunder god and grants command of thunder magic.

table:0FC9A:45: edge holds poison, so a mere wound can
table:0FC9A:48: A legend sword that cuts steel in one blow.
table:0FC9A:49: Even a scaled dragon it can cleave, they
table:0FC9A:52: A holy sword with a special pattern. Its
table:0FC9A:53: wielder gains grace, used by legendary
table:0FC9A:54: hero. It is holy-element and grants Holy
table:0FC9A:56: A sword of the old magic age. A mana-drive
table:0FC9A:57: small magic cannon is fixed to it, firing

table:0FC9A:60: A dark sword forged by Chaos, lord of chaos.

table:0FC9A:64: In the old age when light and dark warred,
table:0FC9A:65: king Sieghart forged this holy sword at the
table:0FC9A:66: cost of his life. It opposes dark magic.
table:0FC9A:68: A small one-handed axe, handy and easy to wield, often used for hunting. Weak as a weapon, but good value for its price.
table:0FC9A:72: A large war axe with a double-edged head on a long haft. Its attack is high, but it is so heavy that a weak person can barely lift it. Attack cost +3.
table:0FC9A:76: A double-edged axe of mithril silver. Its huge head gives fearsome power, yet it is much lighter and easier to wield than other axes. Attack cost +1.
table:0FC9A:80: A cursed axe that brings madness to its wielder and terror to its target. The wielder thinks only of killing and neglects defense. Attack cost +4.
table:0FC9A:84: A huge stone axe carved with runes of destruction and silence. It has fearsome power, while magic reduces its weight. Attack cost +2.
table:0FC9A:88: A workout tool always carried by a muscular bro. It lets him train anywhere. Mediocre as a weapon, but said to help make the bro your friend.
table:0FC9A:92: A primitive striking weapon made from shaped hardwood. Its great weight gives it more power than one might expect. Attack cost +3.
table:0FC9A:96: A mace with a spiked iron ball chained to a long haft. Powerful and hard to dodge, but difficult to wield. Attack cost +2.
table:0FC9A:100: A mace with metal spikes on a long haft. It delivers powerful blows while remaining very easy to wield. Attack cost +2.
table:0FC9A:104: A huge magic hammer that boosts its power
table:0FC9A:105: with willpower. But when willpower runs out,
table:0FC9A:106: its weight hurts. Each hit costs 2 MP for
table:0FC9A:107: AT x1.2. With no MP, x0.8.
table:0FC9A:108: A club said to have been used by Dagda, god of strength. So huge and heavy that an ordinary person cannot even lift it, much less wield it. Attack cost +4.
table:0FC9A:112: Thor's hammer. Thrown, it flies like lightning, crushes its target, and returns. It may also grant Thor's blessing. Attack cost +2.
table:0FC9A:116: A very long cavalry lance used mainly to knock foes from their mounts. A deadly thrust using the mount's speed can pierce even plate armor. Attack cost +3.
table:0FC9A:120: A mithril cavalry lance, lighter and tougher than ordinary lances. Its piercing power delivers devastating thrusts. Attack cost +2. Critical.
table:0FC9A:124: A demonic lance passed among many owners, always drinking blood. Its power is low, but it drains a pierced foe's life and gives it to the wielder. Attack cost +4.
table:0FC9A:128: A magic lance said to embody a fire demon. Eternal flames pour from its tip and haft, granting power over fire magic. Attack cost +2. Critical.
table:0FC9A:132: The lance of a legendary dragon knight. Light, tough, and immensely powerful. It is said to bear the blessing of wind spirits. Attack cost +2. Critical.
table:0FC9A:136: A demon spear seeking battle. It senses war, bursts into flame, and heads for the battlefield. Ordinary people cannot control it. Attack cost +2. Critical.
table:0FC9A:140: Used by mages for defense and also to aid
table:0FC9A:144: A special enchanted staff only a full mage
table:0FC9A:145: may own. It has no weapon power, but it aids
table:0FC9A:148: A lovely enchanted crystal sits on its tip,
table:0FC9A:152: A rod only great mages may own.
table:0FC9A:153: It is built to boost spell effects, so the
table:0FC9A:156: A crystal is set in its tip, and within it
table:0FC9A:157: sleeps a fire demon. A strong caster can
table:0FC9A:160: A staff full of life breath. It boosts the
table:0FC9A:161: holder's healing and grants power over heal
table:0FC9A:164: A staff said used by a legendary archmage.
table:0FC9A:165: boosts spell effects and grants power over
table:0FC9A:168: Mystic staff used by the dragon king as a human.
table:0FC9A:169: A pure-hearted owner can draw its true power.
table:0FC9A:172: A small 80 cm bow. Its range is short, but it is easy to carry. Heavy armor hinders its use and lowers defense. Range 7. Attack cost +10.
table:0FC9A:176: A longbow made of yew. Powerful, penetrating, and long-ranged, but it requires considerable training to master. Range 10. Attack cost +16.
table:0FC9A:180: A magic bow carried by elves, master archers of the forest. It has excellent range but is somewhat bulky. Range 14. Attack cost +20.
table:0FC9A:184: Usually small enough to fit in one hand, this magic bow grows when drawn. A northern god gave it to a prince famed for archery. Range 14. Attack cost +22.
table:0FC9A:188: The bow of Artemis, goddess of the moon. Lunar magic in its arrows maddens foes. Its great size provides exceptional range and power. Range 18. Attack cost +30.
table:0FC9A:192: Full-body armor made from riveted iron plates. Ordinary attacks cannot harm its excellent protection, but its great weight reduces mobility.
table:0FC9A:196: Plate armor of mithril silver, magic metal.
table:0FC9A:197: For plate armor it is very light,
table:0FC9A:200: Mithril silver armor only true knights may
table:0FC9A:201: wear. Countless decorative grooves keep it
table:0FC9A:204: Armor of orichalcum, a rare magic metal.
table:0FC9A:205: It boosts the wearer's aura to guard them,
table:0FC9A:206: so it is far lighter than it looks.

table:0FC9A:218: For its good defense it is cheap, so many

table:0FC9A:228: Legendary armor of Aeneas. No weapon can scratch it. Forged by the smith god, it offers superb defense. Attack cost -3, including mercenaries.
table:0FC9A:232: Armor of thick oil-boiled leather. Somewhat heavy, but far lighter than metal armor and strong enough for even weak wearers.
table:0FC9A:236: A bodybuilder wax. It firms and shines the

table:0FC9A:244: Ordinary clothes with little value as armor. Still, they are better than wearing nothing.
table:0FC9A:248: Clothes of sturdy cloth. Light and loose,
table:0FC9A:250: A protective spell on it guards a little
table:0FC9A:252: A magic belt worn by Thor. Tightening it

table:0FC9A:264: A thin feather robe worn by a celestial maiden. It has high magic resistance, and its wearer can befriend a celestial maiden. Women only.

table:0FC9A:284: Strange magic boots set with white feathers.
table:0FC9A:285: Worn, the body lightens and you run as if

table:0FC9A:292: An accessory with a strange charm power.

table:0FC9A:296: An emerald charm with magic to guard the

table:0FC9A:300: A strange ring set with a gem charged by the

table:0FC9A:303: It also raises a mage's power.
table:0FC9A:304: An amethyst ankh. It protects the whole unit from enemy magic, raises morale, and may trigger a protective spell in battle.
table:0FC9A:308: A gold ring carved with a guard rune. Worn,
table:0FC9A:312: A gold ring carved with a power rune. Worn,

table:0FC9A:320: A beautiful golden crown set with colorful gems. It grants royal dignity and magical authority over people. Attack cost -3, including mercenaries.

table:0FC9A:325: like stars. They aid casting focus and ease

table:0FC9A:336: The Dragon Lord's legendary crown. It grants superb command, and a strong wearer may gain the Dragon King's power. Int +5.
table:0FC9A:340: A silver hairband carved with a wind rune. It is said to let the wearer wield weapons with extraordinary ease. Attack cost -15, including mercenaries.
table:0FC9A:344: A secret stone carved with old runes. They
table:0FC9A:345: remain unread, and it is called Mystery

table:0FC9A:350: The greater the mage, the more they prize

table:0FC9A:356: A crystal sealing a fire demon, with an
table:0FC9A:357: undying flame at its core. It boosts the
table:0FC9A:358: holder's magic and resists fire too.
table:0FC9A:360: A mystical charm bestowed by heaven's holy king upon those who do good. A unit led by its bearer gains holy power and strong will. Int +15.
table:0FC9A:364: A charm made from the pure white feathers
table:0FC9A:365: dropped as six angels flew. Its huge magic
table:0FC9A:366: defense makes it the best charm. Priests
table:0FC9A:368: The light goddess's holy ring. It maximizes magic, shields the unit from spells, and embodies part of her power. Range +3, area +2.

table:0FC9A:373: Dark power raises strength and magic defense,
